name: The White House
subject: Celebrating Achievement, Planning for Prosperity
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=bc38c24e1ba757369d01001f9d0040d112fb7844d7d463d3e5103bf9b337535075b06a1dbb3366b23c3e5a67b75346b78e82af1109e9c219

Cutting Through the Spin on Tax Reform

Denouncing Republican tax reform as a giveaway to large corporations is a blatant misdirection, says the Wall Street Journal’s editorial board. “The irony is that the Senate and House bills would do far more to stop corporate tax gaming than anything the Obama Administration did in eight years.”

“The truth is that it’s a serious attempt to fix a broken U.S. code that has festered for years and made America increasingly uncompetitive as a destination for mobile global capital,” they write. “The GOP reforms would help the economy and make it harder for corporations to avoid paying taxes.”

Read the full article
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=bc38c24e1ba7573692d23bf634ceeb4fd1aa8f03eb43d52cb97bd07d8ec89def94c568365d01ce0fad8c553396923d0b791a6896f27b8a9f
here.

National Thanksgiving Turkeys Have Arrived

Yesterday, two Thanksgiving turkeys arrived at The Willard Hotel in Washington, D.C., where they are currently being pampered in preparation for the Presidential turkey pardoning tomorrow afternoon. One turkey will receive a pardon and the title of National Thanksgiving Turkey, while the other will serve as an official alternate should the pardoned turkey be unable to fulfill his duties.

This morning, the names of the two turkeys were announced – Drumstick and Wishbone.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=bc38c24e1ba7573632ae6eb36d96f6a9323027ddfbb760db7e40e2a86e8a6509f8ea06430926be665b06f23d5cf33b51cacf6fea01e4d2ff
VOTE NOW to decide which turkey President Trump should pardon!

Following the ceremony, the turkeys will join last year’s turkeys at Virginia Tech’s “Gobblers Rest” exhibit in Blacksburg, Virginia, where students and veterinarians care for the turkeys, and the public can visit and learn about the university’s teaching, research, and outreach programs in animal and poultry sciences and veterinary medicine.

NCAA Champions Visit the White House

On Friday, President Donald J. Trump hosted 18 NCAA championship teams at the White House. The President commended the student athletes for their extraordinary athletic achievements and their diligence in balancing a commitment to sport and excellence in the classroom.

The teams in attendance were: Oklahoma Men's Golf,
Oklahoma Women's Gymnastics,
Oklahoma Men's Gymnastics,
Maryland Women's Lacrosse,
Maryland Men's Lacrosse,
West Virginia Rifle (Coed),
University of Washington Women's Rowing,
Penn State Women's Rugby,
Penn State Men's Wrestling (Delegation),
Oklahoma Softball,
Virginia Men's Tennis,
Arizona State Women's Triathlon,
Florida Baseball,
McKendree Women's Bowling,
Texas A&M Men's Indoor Track and Field,
Ohio State Men's Volleyball, and
Utah Skiing.

See highlights from the event
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=bc38c24e1ba757363ac29f6bed167bc21bdc78135d82eeb052b9b75f6de602f58897621c75b341335d3e7fac1f10b8ea77c2ace7b5724111
here.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and the University of Oklahoma Softball NCAA Women's College World Series Champion Team | November 17, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks)

POTUS TODAY

This morning, President Trump received his daily intelligence briefing followed by a meeting with his Cabinet. Afterwards, the President had lunch with Vice President Mike Pence and Secretary of Housing and Urban Development Ben Carson. Later, the President met with Secretary of State Rex Tillerson and U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations Nikki Haley.

This afternoon, First Lady Melania Trump will participate in the Christmas Tree Presentation at the White House. The tree will be displayed in the Blue Room and will be presented by the National Christmas Tree Association.

COMING UP

Tomorrow, President Trump will participate in the Presentation of the National Thanksgiving Turkey.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=bc38c24e1ba7573694b73e09a9c88ff6d3cbc455a3475fdf55903b0787bb2a234f2854086cade5b3caaadce90033cb461367796c7359227c
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=bc38c24e1ba75736d9e32cc064e9aa8707b4872711fd0fa711a1a84c23aa2e27e4ec5047d27082998252d307d33dd6620d428b70fd74804b
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4edc28397f1c1edf5336b6f59cc7f3f4b66155e7f6c29a78e52238d18cd626dfa19410eb3950ad705422cf88aaaded4c7

subject: Got your turkey in the brine yet?
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail
body:
| |
---
| |
---
| You're not dreaming, we promise. It's already November. Thanksgiving is Thursday. If you're planning to soak your turkey in brine, you'd better get on it. There is one more thing you can do to make sure you're all ready to relax on Thursday -- [_**give $5, $10, or more to make sure we reach our $11,000 November goal.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e77/622b7b5b/78b8503/307baa3d/3619897907/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse171120nd?refcode=dwse171120nd_m&recurring=true&amount=5") As much as we'd all like to take a month off, we can't. There is just too much to do. Debbie is working to make sure all of our priorities don't fall to the Trump administration. She's doing her best to hold Trump and Republicans in Congress accountable for their wrong-headed actions. It's a pretty big job. And don't forget her Flashy Four opponents in Florida, attacking her any chance they get. Debbie needs us to fight beside her, to hit goals and organize. We can't afford to miss a goal -- not even in November. And we need to stay on track. [_**Please help us make sure that we reach this goal. Give whatever amount you can. Then you can enjoy this Thanksgiving break, knowing you did your part to build the best grassroots team in the country.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e77/622b7b5b/78b8503/307baa3d/3619897907/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse171120nd?refcode=dwse171120nd_m&recurring=true&amount=5") Thanks! There is one more thing you can do to make sure you're all ready to relax on Thursday -- "" As much as we'd all like to take a month off, we can't. There is just too much to do. Debbie is working to make sure all of our priorities don't fall to the Trump administration. She's doing her best to hold Trump and Republicans in Congress accountable for their wrong-headed actions. It's a pretty big job. And don't forget her Flashy Four opponents in Florida, attacking her any chance they get. Debbie needs us to fight beside her, to hit goals and organize. We can't afford to miss a goal -- not even in November. And we need to stay on track. "" Thanks! subject: ICYMI - GOP official in NH urges Bill Clinton’s name be dropped from state Democratic dinner
body:
**ICYMI** \- This weekend, New Hampshire Republican State Committee Chair Jeanie Forrester was quoted in Fox News regarding the New Hampshire Democrat Party honoring sexual predator Bill Clinton in the name of their main fundraising dinner.

_Hours before the event, state Republican Party Chairwoman Jeanie Forrester took aim at the New Hampshire Democratic dinner.___
_“Tonight, New Hampshire Democrats will gather to honor the President Clinton. At a time when Harvey Weinstein and Senator Al Franken have had credible allegations of sexual assault, and as liberals across the country start to finally denounce Bill Clinton for his past lewd behavior, the New Hampshire Democrats should immediately denounce and remove the name of a sexual predator from their main fundraising dinner,” Forrester wrote._
**New Hampshire Democrat Chair Ray Buckley completely dodged the question: **
_Asked by Fox News if the state Democratic party should have a conversation about taking Clinton’s name off the dinner, party Chairman Raymond Buckley said, “I think it would be an interesting conversation right after we see the resignation of Donald Trump.”_
**No other Democrat had the courage to speak on the record about them honoring a sexual predator: **
_No other state party officials or Democratic activists at the dinner wanted to talk on the record about whether Clinton’s name should be removed from the dinner. But the issue could possibly come up when the state party holds a major meeting early next month._

name: Keren, Megan, Jenny, and Jess
subject: A message from the women of Team Kaine
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Hi there,

This is an email from Keren Charles Dongo, Megan Apper, Jenny Nadicksbernd, and Jess Reid. We're Tim's campaign manager, research director, finance director, and digital director, and we wanted to write to his supporters today to talk to you a little bit about why we're proud to work for him.

Over the past few weeks and months, the news has been dominated by accounts of rampant sexual harassment, assault, and abuse in Hollywood, media, politics, and communities across the country. It's been exhausting to keep up with each new story. The allegations are disgusting, and many of the reactions we see are disheartening.

But alongside those disappointing reactions, there have also been people who are stepping up. People who are using their platforms to say that enough is enough. And there has been one strong reaction that has been particularly gratifying for us to see: from our boss, Tim Kaine. Earlier this month, Tim published [a guest column on Esquire.com](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=NwsdYyTPcW1wkP9Lfa3WoZW2ZTbkrMki) titled, "Why Men Need to Step Up for Women's Equality--and How I Plan to Do My Part." It begins like this:

_For a country that does so many things well, we 're not doing nearly enough to address the fact that women still do not have a full and equal role in our society, and that sexism, sexual harassment, and sexual assault are pervasive problems._

The column goes on to detail many of the inequalities facing women today, and it ends with a personal pledge to forcefully condemn sexual harassment and assault, to continue to advocate for women leaders, and to support women everywhere who share their stories.

This commitment means a lot to us. It reminds us that not only is Tim an advocate and leader for us -- he's also a great leader for women across Virginia and America. And we need that. Because the fact is, men are critical allies in the fight for true equality. We can't achieve it without their support and, more importantly, their actions.

**We are proud to stand with Tim, and today, we're asking you to join us.[Add your name to ours, and say you're ready to keep fighting against sexism, sexual harassment, and sexual assault.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=S5j2jRNTlQM%2Fvt7cBdNVhJW2ZTbkrMki)**

Thank you.

Keren, Megan, Jenny, and Jess

Keren Charles Dongo, Campaign Manager
Megan Apper, Research Director
Jenny Nadicksbernd, Finance Director
Jessalyn Reid, Digital Director
| [**DONATE**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=n3dFPWoC9kgsYHUfJnhcz5W2ZTbkrMki)
---
| Paid for by Kaine for Virginia

subject: RE: Climate Deniers...

body:
Sign your name -- we need you on this list.

| [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MTI0MDE3NTAS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MTg5NDI4NQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Friend, Did you see this? Fighting climate change is more important than ever. We need clean energy and international cooperation, and we need it now. We've already gathered over 9,000 names, but we're missing yours, friend. [**Sign on now to tell Donald Trump and other climate deniers -- they can 't ignore science.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTI0MDE3NTAS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MTg5NDI4NQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Very truly yours, Jay Inslee
\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Jay Inslee
Date: Wednesday, Nov 15, 2017
Subject: You'll never guess who Trump sent to Bonn. Friend, Donald Trump's contribution to the UN Climate Conference in Bonn, Germany? A pro-coal panel led by fossil fuel promoters. At a _climate change_ conference. So we showed up to keep them honest. Oregon Gov. Kate Brown and I brought a clear message to the press at their panel: While Trump denies climate change, we are moving forward to a clean energy future. On the West Coast, we're building a Great Blue Wall that puts people and our climate ahead of the big-coal and big-oil lobby. [**Join us: Add your name to speak out against Donald Trump 's denial of climate science.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTI0MDE3NTAS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MTg5NDI4NQS2&mt=1&rt=0) [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTI0MDE3NTAS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MTg5NDI4NQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Every single person I talked to in Bonn was more energized than ever to take on climate action. It's a shame that the White House isn't harnessing that energy -- but where they fall short in leadership, we'll stand up to prove our strength. When confronted with anti-science lies that threaten our world's future, it's our moral responsibility to speak the truth, loudly and forcefully. And it's our responsibility to act. That's what we did in Bonn. That's what we're doing in Washington state -- and across the country with the U.S. Climate Alliance, a partnership with 14 of my fellow governors collectively representing almost 40% of the U.S. economy. [**So stand with us, and stand with the world: Sign on now to reject Donald Trump 's denial of climate science through action.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTI0MDE3NTAS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MTg5NDI4NQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Donald Trump can't stop us. We aren't slowing down one bit on our work to move towards a clean energy economy, no matter who's whispering in Trump's ear. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [SIGN ON »](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MTI0MDE3NTAS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MTg5NDI4NQS2&mt=1&rt=0)

Every single person I talked to in Bonn was more energized than ever to take on climate action. It's a shame that the White House isn't harnessing that energy -- but where they fall short in leadership, we'll stand up to prove our strength. When confronted with anti-science lies that threaten our world's future, it's our moral responsibility to speak the truth, loudly and forcefully. And it's our responsibility to act. That's what we did in Bonn. That's what we're doing in Washington state -- and across the country with the U.S. Climate Alliance, a partnership with 14 of my fellow governors collectively representing almost 40% of the U.S. economy. Donald Trump can't stop us. We aren't slowing down one bit on our work to move towards a clean energy economy, no matter who's whispering in Trump's ear. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee |

|
[](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/120308d/2fde7c66/44eece18/15699d0d/1796767315/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTciLCJKU1ZNUVZOVVRrRk5SU1VsIjoiIn0=/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/stand-against-the-gops-middle-class-tax-hike?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&lastname=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em17_171120_Taxes_adv_gq_ful__RC&source=em17_171120_Taxes_adv_gq_ful__RC")

Friend,

When I ran for governor, I said we needed to end the tax giveaways for the wealthy and corporations and focus more on giving middle-class families a break.

Last week, Republicans in the U.S. House of Representatives passed their $5.5 trillion tax bill -- a massive tax giveaway for the wealthy and corporations.

Here's what you need to know: more than 700,000 North Carolina households earning less than $136,000 will see a tax hike.

**[This is a bad bill, and if it becomes law, it will hurt middle-class North Carolina families. Add your name now to stand against it.](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/120308d/2fde7c66/44eece18/15699d0c/1796767315/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTciLCJKU1ZNUVZOVVRrRk5SU1VsIjoiIn0=/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/stand-against-the-gops-middle-class-tax-hike?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&lastname=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=2+-+This+is+a+bad+bill+and+if+it+becomes+law&utm_campaign=em17_171120_Taxes_adv_gq_ful__RC&source=em17_171120_Taxes_adv_gq_ful__RC")**

Our state needs help from the federal government to fight the opioid epidemic, recover from natural disasters like Hurricane Matthew, and boost stagnant middle-class wages.

This tax giveaway to the wealthy will only set us back and won't help us solve any of these problems.

This bill would be bad for North Carolina, and we should stand against it.

**[Add your name if you're with me.](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/120308d/2fde7c66/44eece18/15699d0f/1796767315/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTciLCJKU1ZNUVZOVVRrRk5SU1VsIjoiIn0=/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/stand-against-the-gops-middle-class-tax-hike?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&lastname=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=3+-+Add+your+name+if+youre+with+me&utm_campaign=em17_171120_Taxes_adv_gq_ful__RC&source=em17_171120_Taxes_adv_gq_ful__RC")**

Thanks for all you do.

body:

Friend,

There’s a lot of talk in Washington about tax cuts.

The House has a bill. The Senate has a bill. They both have their talking points and press tours, and the Washington politicians and fake news media have been talking about it non-stop.
